member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Budget.BudgetReportServiceTests
member.summary: Contains unit tests for the BudgetReportService, verifying the generation of raw report data for a full month.
member.remarks: These tests cover a wide range of budget and posting scenarios, including grouped budget purposes, budgets with both positive and negative expectations, fulfilled and unfulfilled budgets, overruns, and postings without budgeted expectations. The tests ensure that the BudgetReportService correctly aggregates, categorizes, and reports financial data based on the defined budgets and actual postings, including the handling of unbudgeted and uncategorized transactions.

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Infrastructure.Budget.ReportCacheServiceTests
member.summary: Tests for cache invalidation behaviors.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Infrastructure.Budget.ReportCacheServiceTests.MarkBudgetReportCacheEntriesForUpdateAsync_ShouldMarkEntry_WhenBookingRangeInsideCacheRange
member.summary: Marks cache entries for update when the booking range is inside the cached range.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Infrastructure.Budget.ReportCacheServiceTests.MarkBudgetReportCacheEntriesForUpdateAsync_ShouldNotMarkEntry_WhenBookingRangeOutsideCacheRange
member.summary: Does not mark cache entries when the booking range is outside the cached range or cache key prefix differs.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Infrastructure.Budget.ReportCacheServiceTests.MarkBudgetReportCacheEntriesForUpdateAsync_ShouldMarkOverlappingMonths_WhenBookingRangeCrossesMonths
member.summary: Marks overlapping monthly cache entries when a booking range spans multiple months.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Reports.ReportAggregationServiceTests.SeedAllKindsAsync(FinanceManager.Infrastructure.AppDbContext,System.Guid,System.DateTime,System.Int32)
member.summary: Seeds two accounts, two contacts, two savings plans, two securities and creates monthly aggregates for the last N months. For each entity and month two postings are added (1st and 15th), with a global amount counter starting at 1.00€ and increasing by 0.01€ per posting to ensure uniqueness. Returns the created entities, the list of months and a sum lookup for expected assertions.
Hilfsfunktion: Erwartete Monats?Summe je Entität und Vergleichswerte (Vormonat, Vorjahr) aus Seed?Lookup berechnen.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Reports.ReportAggregationServiceTests.QueryAsync_Monthly_AllKinds_VerifyPrevAndYear_ForTwoEntities
member.summary: Seeds 24 months for 2 accounts/contacts/savings/securities with unique amounts (1.00€ + 0.01€ per posting), two postings per month (1st/15th). Builds a monthly report for Bank, Contact, SavingsPlan, Security with comparisons (prev + year) and verifies the latest month's balances for both entities. Expected: Amount equals seeded monthly sum; Previous equals exact previous month; YearAgo equals same month last year.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Reports.ReportAggregationServiceTests.QueryAsync_Monthly_SelectOtherEntities_ShouldBeEmpty
member.summary: With the same seeded postings, query with filters that select non-existing entities. Expect an empty result (no rows).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Reports.ReportAggregationServiceTests.QueryAsync_VariousIntervals_Bank_VerifySums(FinanceManager.Shared.Dtos.Reports.ReportInterval)
member.summary: Quarterly/HalfYearly/Yearly/YTD/AllHistory: Verify that the aggregation sums match the seeded per-month sums grouped per interval for Bank postings. Previous comparisons are checked for the exact previous interval when applicable, YearAgo for yearly. For AllHistory, verify total across all returned periods equals the seeded total.

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests
member.summary: Tests for covering FIFO cost basis calculations.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_ReturnEmpty_For_NoTransactions
member.summary: An empty transaction list must return a zeroed result with no lots and no warning.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_SetTotalCostBasis_For_SingleBuy
member.summary: A single buy of 10 shares at €100 each sets TotalCostBasis=1000 and creates one lot.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_ApplyFifoCostBasis_After_PartialSell
member.summary: Buy 10@100, Buy 5@120, Sell 8 → FIFO removes first 8 shares from lot1. Remaining: 2@100 + 5@120.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_AddFeeToLotCostBasis_When_SameGroupId
member.summary: A Fee with the same GroupId as a Buy is added to that lot's cost basis. Buy 10@100 (cost=1000) on day 1 + Fee 20 with same groupId on day 2 → lot cost = 1020, CostPerUnit = 102. The fee is placed on a later date to guarantee it is processed after the buy (FIFO sort order).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_HandleOversell_WithWarning
member.summary: Selling more shares than are held sets HasOversellWarning=true and clamps shares to 0. Buy 5, Sell 10 → HasOversellWarning = true, TotalSharesHeld = 0.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_IgnoreDividendsAndTax_InCostBasis
member.summary: Dividends and taxes must not change the cost basis or the lot count. Buy 10@100, Dividend 50, Tax −10 → cost basis unchanged at 1000.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_SortByDate_ThenById
member.summary: When two Buy transactions share the same date, the FIFO queue is filled in ascending Id order (deterministic), and a subsequent Sell removes shares from the correct first-in lot.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_Handle_MultipleFullSells
member.summary: Buy 10@100, Sell 5@110, Sell 5@120 → realized gains = 5×10 + 5×20 = 150.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_ReturnZeroSharesHeld_After_FullSell
member.summary: After a full sell, no shares are held and no lots remain.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_NotCreateLot_When_BuyQuantityIsNull
member.summary: A buy transaction with a null quantity must not create a lot. The result should have zero shares held and empty lots.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_NotCreateLot_When_BuyQuantityIsZero
member.summary: A buy transaction with quantity zero must not create a lot.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_SkipSell_When_SellQuantityIsNull
member.summary: A sell transaction with a null quantity must be silently skipped.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_NotCrash_When_FeeHasNoMatchingBuyLot
member.summary: A standalone fee (GroupId does not match any Buy lot) must be included in TotalCostBasis. This reflects Option-A: standalone fees always contribute to invested capital, regardless of whether they were linked via GroupId.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_NotCrash_When_FeeLinkedToFullySoldLot
member.summary: A fee linked to a fully-sold lot must not crash. The lot is gone after the sell, but the fee arrives later.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_AccumulateFees_When_MultipleFeesShareSameGroupId
member.summary: Multiple fees referencing the same buy lot via GroupId must accumulate on the lot's cost basis. Buy 10 @ 100 = 1000 + fee1 (5) + fee2 (3) → TotalCostBasis = 1008.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_IncludeStandaloneFeesInTotalCostBasis
member.summary:
Regression test for the standalone-fee bug: Fees without a matching GroupId (standalone) must be included in TotalCostBasis so that invested capital is never under-reported when fees were recorded without a GroupId link.
Scenario: Buy1 (1 000 €, 10 shares, groupId=A) + Fee1 (20 €, same groupId=A) → lot cost = 1 020 (linked) Buy2 ( 600 €, 5 shares, groupId=B) + Fee2 (15 €, random groupId) → standalone fee Expected TotalCostBasis = 1020 + 600 + 15 = 1635 Expected StandaloneFeeTotal = 15

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_SetOversellWarning_When_SellExceedsAllLots
member.summary: A sell that exceeds all available lots must set the oversell warning flag. After an oversell, TotalSharesHeld must not go below zero.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_CreateNewLot_When_BuyAfterFullSell
member.summary: After a complete sell-out (0 shares remaining), a new buy creates a fresh lot as if starting from scratch.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.FifoCostBasisCalculatorTests.Calculate_Should_ProcessBuyBeforeSell_When_SameDateAndBuyIdIsSmaller
member.summary: When a buy and a sell share the same date, the buy (lower Id) is processed first due to the ThenBy(Id) sort — so the sell does not trigger an oversell warning.

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests
member.summary: Tests for covering cache miss, cache hit and invalidation behaviour.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a fresh cache instance for each test.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.GetOrCreateAsync_Should_CallFactory_On_CacheMiss
member.summary: On a cache miss the factory delegate is called and its result is returned.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.GetOrCreateAsync_Should_ReturnCachedValue_On_CacheHit
member.summary: On a cache hit the factory is NOT called a second time; the cached value is returned.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.InvalidateAsync_Should_RemoveCachedEntries_WithMatchingPrefix
member.summary: InvalidateAsync removes all cached entries whose key contains the given prefix substring. After invalidation, the next call to GetOrCreateAsync triggers the factory again.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.InvalidateAsync_Should_NotRemoveEntries_WithDifferentPrefix
member.summary: InvalidateAsync must NOT remove entries whose key does not contain the given prefix. Entries for a different security remain in the cache.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.GetOrCreateAsync_Should_NotCacheResult_When_FactoryReturnsNull
member.summary: When the factory returns null the result must NOT be cached. A second call must invoke the factory again.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.GetOrCreateAsync_Should_CallFactoryAgain_When_EntryHasExpired
member.summary: An entry with a very short TTL must expire and trigger the factory again.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.InvalidateAsync_Should_NotThrow_When_CacheIsEmpty
member.summary: InvalidateAsync must not throw when the cache is empty.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisCacheTests.GetOrCreateAsync_Should_NotThrow_When_CalledConcurrently
member.summary: Concurrent calls with the same key must not throw (race condition is a known limitation, but no exception may be raised — BUG-3: factory may be called more than once).

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests
member.summary: Integration tests for covering user-scoping, security ownership, and orchestration of return calculations. Uses EF Core InMemory database with a fresh instance per test.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a fresh DB and SUT for each test.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.SetupSecurityAndUser
member.summary: Creates a security and its owning user, persists both and returns them.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.CreateBuyPosting(System.Guid,System.DateTime,System.Decimal,System.Decimal)
member.summary: Creates a Buy-type for the given security without persisting it.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetReturnSummaryAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_SecurityDoesNotBelongToUser
member.summary: When the security does not belong to the requesting user, the method must return null (user-scoping S-1 requirement).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetReturnSummaryAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_NoTransactionsExist
member.summary: When the security is found and owned by the user but has no transactions, the method must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetReturnSummaryAsync_Should_SetHasMissingPrices_When_NoCurrentPriceAvailable
member.summary: When there are transactions but no current price data available, HasMissingPrices must be true and MissingPricesHint must be set.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetReturnSummaryAsync_Should_SetOversellHint_When_OversellWarningActive
member.summary: When the FIFO result signals an oversell warning, HasMissingPrices must be true and MissingPricesHint must be populated with the oversell warning message.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetReturnSummaryAsync_Should_IncludeSalesProceedsNetOfFees_When_FullSellCycleExists
member.summary:
Full buy–sell cycle using the real . One buy with linked fee (GroupId A) is fully liquidated by a sell with linked fee (GroupId B).
Transactions (exact values from production database, Fraport AG): Buy 25 shares −957,75 EUR (GroupId A) Fee −7,29 EUR (GroupId A – linked to buy, added to cost basis by FIFO) Fee −7,78 EUR (GroupId B – linked to sell, deducted from sales proceeds) Sell 25 shares +1151,50 EUR (GroupId B, Quantity stored as −25 in domain model)
Expected: TotalSharesHeld = 0 (all sold) CurrentMarketValue = 0 (no remaining shares × price) InvestedCapital = 0 (FIFO cost basis of CURRENTLY HELD shares; fully sold → 0) historicalInvestedCap = 965,04 (internal only; buy 957,75 + buy-fee 7,29 → used for return calc) salesProceeds net = 1143,72 (sell 1151,50 − sell-fee 7,78) TotalReturnAbsolute = 1143,72 − 965,04 = 178,68

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetSparklineDataAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_NoTransactionsExist
member.summary: When there are no transactions for the security, the method must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetSparklineDataAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_LessThan30PricePoints
member.summary: When there are fewer than 30 price points available after the first transaction date, the method must return null (not enough data for a meaningful sparkline).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetSparklineDataAsync_Should_ReturnSparkline_When_Exactly30PricePoints
member.summary: When there are at least 30 price points, the method must return a non-null with data points.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetCashflowTimelineAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_SecurityNotFound
member.summary: When the security does not exist or does not belong to the requesting user, the method must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetCashflowTimelineAsync_Should_ReturnEmptyDto_When_NoTransactions
member.summary: When the security exists and is owned by the user but there are no transactions, the method must return an empty (non-null) DTO with empty collections. This differs from other methods that return null for empty data.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetBenchmarkComparisonAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_NoBenchmarkConfigured
member.summary: When no benchmark security is configured for the user (BenchmarkSecurityId is null), the method must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetBenchmarkComparisonAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_BenchmarkSecurityNotOwnedByUser
member.summary: When the configured benchmark security belongs to a different user, the method must return null (ownership enforcement S-3).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.UpdateUserSettingsAsync_Should_ThrowArgumentException_When_BenchmarkSecurityNotOwnedByUser
member.summary: When a benchmark security ID is provided but the security belongs to a different user, the method must throw (S-3 ownership enforcement).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.UpdateUserSettingsAsync_Should_ClearBenchmark_When_BenchmarkIdIsNull
member.summary: When benchmarkSecurityId is null, the existing benchmark setting is cleared without error.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.InvalidateCacheAsync_Should_InvalidateCorrectToken_When_CalledWithValidIds
member.summary: InvalidateCacheAsync must pass a token that contains both the securityId and userId to the cache's InvalidateAsync method, so all related entries are evicted.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetDetailedMetricsAsync_Should_ReturnNullSharpeRatio_When_ShowSharpeRatioIsFalse
member.summary: When the user has ShowSharpeRatio = false, the Sharpe Ratio must not be computed and the result must be null (opt-in guard FR-8).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetDetailedMetricsAsync_Should_ReturnNullSharpeRatio_When_VolatilityIsZero
member.summary: When volatility is zero (flat price series), the Sharpe Ratio must remain null to avoid division by zero, even when ShowSharpeRatio is enabled.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_SecurityNotFound
member.summary: When the security does not exist or does not belong to the requesting user, the method must return null (user-scoping S-1).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_ReturnNull_When_NoTransactions
member.summary: When the security is owned by the user but has no transactions, the method must return null (no data to break down).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_ReturnAllKpis_When_TransactionsExist
member.summary: When there are transactions, the method must return a non-null list containing breakdowns for all widget KPIs (TotalReturn, MarketValue, InvestedCapital, Cagr, Irr).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_PopulateMetadata_For_EachKpi
member.summary: Each returned breakdown must have a non-empty display name, formula text, and description.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_IncludeBuyPostings_In_InvestedCapitalBreakdown
member.summary: The InvestedCapital breakdown must contain a "Käufe" group with one item per buy posting and the group total must equal the sum of all buy amounts.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_IncludeDividends_In_TotalReturnBreakdown
member.summary: The TotalReturn breakdown must include a "Dividenden (netto)" group (Group[2]) containing each dividend posting with the correct amount.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_BreakDownTotalReturn_Into_FourGroups
member.summary: The TotalReturn breakdown must use the new four-group structure: [0] "Aktueller Marktwert" (positiv), [1] "Investiertes Kapital" (negativ), [2] "Dividenden (netto)" (positiv), [3] "Gesamtrendite" (Ergebnis). Group[0] must have one item with the current market value. Group[1] must have the buy items negated (outflows).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_PopulateItems_For_FeesGroup
member.summary: Fees in TotalReturn must appear inside Group[1] "Investiertes Kapital" as negated items (standalone fees with note "Gebühr (ohne Kaufzuordnung)"), not in a separate fees group. This is a regression test ensuring individual fee rows are always visible in the info panel.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_Should_ListEachBuyAndFeeAsSeperateItem_In_InvestedCapitalBreakdown
member.summary: The InvestedCapital and TotalReturn breakdowns must list each buy posting and each linked fee (same GroupId) as separate items. Fees with a GroupId that does not match any buy (standalone) must be appended at the end of the "Käufe" group with the note "Gebühr (ohne Kaufzuordnung)".

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Should_HaveExactlyFiveGroups_In_CorrectOrder
member.summary: The CAGR breakdown must contain exactly 5 groups in the correct order: Marktwert, Nettodividenden, Investiertes Kapital, Anlagedauer, CAGR (Ergebnis).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group0_Should_ContainMarktwertItems
member.summary: Group[0] "Marktwert" must contain exactly one item representing total shares held × current price – identical to TotalReturn's Marktwert group.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group1_Should_ContainNetDividendItems
member.summary: Group[1] "Nettodividenden" must contain dividend items and tax items (negative). The GroupTotal must equal gross dividends minus taxes.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group2_Should_ContainInvestedCapitalItems
member.summary: Group[2] "Investiertes Kapital" must contain the buy items (positive amounts) and their fees. The GroupTotal must equal the total cost basis (investedCapital).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group3_Should_HaveGroupNoteWithDuration
member.summary: Group[3] "Anlagedauer" must have a non-null GroupNote with the holding period text and must contain three text-only items (Erster Kauf, Heute, Zeitraum).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group4_Should_HaveGroupTotalPercent
member.summary: Group[4] "CAGR (Ergebnis)" must have GroupTotalPercent set to the computed CAGR value and must have no items (result-only group).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group4_Should_ReturnZero_When_YearsIsZero
member.summary: Edge case: when the holding period in years is 0 (today is the first buy day), the CAGR result group must have GroupTotalPercent = 0 (not null, not error).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Group4_Should_ReturnZero_When_InvestedCapitalIsZero
member.summary: Edge case: when there are no buy transactions (historicalInvestedCapital = 0), the CAGR result group must have GroupTotalPercent = 0 (no division by zero). With no buys, firstBuyDate = Today → cagrYears = 0, which also triggers the guard.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_CagrBreakdown_Should_HaveCorrectFormulaText
member.summary: The CAGR FormulaText must contain the complete formula as specified.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_Should_HaveExactlyTwoGroups
member.summary: The IRR breakdown must contain exactly 2 groups: [0] "Cashflows (Eingabe für IRR-Berechnung)" and [1] "IRR (Ergebnis)".

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_CashflowItems_ShouldBeChronologicallySorted
member.summary: The cashflow timeline items must be sorted chronologically (oldest first), with the terminal market-value entry last (today's date).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_BuysMustAppearAsNegativeAmounts
member.summary: Each buy posting must appear as a negative cashflow item (Mittelabfluss) in the timeline.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_DividendsMustAppearAsNetPositiveAmounts
member.summary: Each dividend must appear as a net-positive cashflow (gross minus linked taxes) in the timeline.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_LastItemMustBeCurrentMarketValue
member.summary: The last item in the cashflow timeline must be the current market value with today's date.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_ResultGroup_ShouldHaveGroupTotalPercent
member.summary: The IRR result group (Group[1]) must carry the computed IRR as GroupTotalPercent.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_FormulaText_ShouldContainKeyTerms
member.summary: The IRR FormulaText must contain "IRR" and the summation symbol "Σ" or the key term "Cashflow".

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_TimelineGroupNote_ShouldContainIrrRate
member.summary: When IRR is computable the GroupNote of the timeline group must contain the formatted IRR rate so the user can read "r = X,XX %".

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_ItemsWithIrr_ShouldHaveDiscountFields
member.summary: When IRR is computable each timeline item must have YearsSinceT0, DiscountFactor, and OriginalCashflow populated.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_FirstItem_DiscountFactorShouldBeOne
member.summary: The first cashflow item is at t₀, so its discount factor must equal 1.0 exactly.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_ItemAmount_ShouldEqualDiscountedCashflow
member.summary: The Amount of each item must approximately equal OriginalCashflow / (1+r)^t.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetKpiBreakdownsAsync_IrrBreakdown_SumOfPresentValues_ShouldBeApproxZero
member.summary: Group[1] "Summe der Barwerte (Probe)" must have GroupTotal approximately equal to 0 when a valid IRR is used (by definition of IRR the sum of present values = 0).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPerformanceChartDataAsync_Should_ShowZeroMarketValue_AfterFullSell
member.summary:
After a full sell of all shares, all chart data points for dates on or after the sell date must have a portfolio value of 0 (no shares held).
Regression guard for a sign-convention bug: domain sell quantities are stored as negative values (e.g. −10). When ComputeSharesHeldOnDate subtracts a negative quantity (shares -= −10), shares are incorrectly increased instead of decreased, causing the market value to double rather than drop to zero.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPerformanceChartDataAsync_Should_SimulatePrices_WhenNoPriceHistoryExists
member.summary:
When no real price history exists for a security, ComputePerformanceChartDataAsync must simulate prices from buy/sell transaction amounts. The implied unit price for each transaction is |Amount| / |Quantity|. Between two anchor points (e.g., buy price and sell price), the price is linearly interpolated for each calendar day.
Example: buy 10 shares for 100€ on 15.01 (price = 10€); sell 10 shares for 150€ on 20.01 (price = 15€). Expected simulated prices: 15.01→10€, 16.01→11€, 17.01→12€, 18.01→13€, 19.01→14€, 20.01→15€. Portfolio values are shares × price (0 after sell).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPerformanceChartDataAsync_Should_SimulatePrices_ForOwnershipPeriod_WhenRealPricesStartAfterSell
member.summary:
When real price history only exists AFTER the last sell (i.e., during the ownership period there are no prices), the chart must still show simulated prices for the ownership period. The post-sell data points must show portfolio value 0 because no shares are held regardless of the real price.
Scenario: buy Jan 15 (10 shares, 100€ → 10€/share), sell Jan 20 (150€ → 15€/share), first real price on Jan 25 (20€). The Jan 15-19 period must be simulated.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPerformanceChartDataAsync_Should_SimulatePrices_ForGapBeforeFirstRealPrice_WhenRealPricesStartMidOwnership
member.summary:
When real price history starts WITHIN the ownership period (after the buy but before the sell), the chart must simulate prices for the gap before the first real price and then use real prices for the remainder. This ensures a continuous chart from the first buy onwards.
Scenario: buy Jan 1 (10 shares, 100€ → 10€/share), first real price Jan 11 (25€), sell Jan 21 (300€ → 30€/share). The Jan 1-10 period must use simulated prices; Jan 11+ must use the real price (25€, NOT the simulated ~15€).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPeriodicReturnsAsync_Should_StopAtLastTransactionYear_When_PositionFullyClosed
member.summary: When a security was bought in year A and sold in year B (B < current year), the result must contain entries only for years A through B, not beyond. A fully closed position must not produce empty years after the sale.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPeriodicReturnsAsync_Should_ReturnPositiveAnnualReturn_When_StockAppreciatedInBuyYear
member.summary: In the year of the first purchase (starting portfolio value = 0), the Modified Dietz formula must return a meaningful return (positive when stock appreciated, negative when it declined) instead of 0 as the old simple-HPR formula produced.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPeriodicReturnsAsync_Should_ReturnPositiveAnnualReturn_When_SoldWithGainInSellYear
member.summary: In the year of a full sale (ending portfolio value = 0), the Modified Dietz formula must produce a positive return when the sell proceeds exceed the start-of-year market value minus fees.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnAnalysisServiceTests.GetPeriodicReturnsAsync_Should_ProduceMonthlyReturn_When_PositionOpenedMidMonth
member.summary: Monthly returns must not be null for months where the position was held, even if the position was opened with a buy in that same month (starting value = 0).

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests
member.summary: Tests for covering all financial math methods.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTotalReturn_Should_ReturnPositive_When_MarketValueExceedsInvested
member.summary: When the market value exceeds the invested capital (plus dividends), the return is positive. Invested=1000, MarketValue=1200, Dividends=50 → (1200+50−1000)/1000 = 0.25 = 25 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTotalReturn_Should_ReturnNegative_When_MarketValueBelowInvested
member.summary: When the market value is below the invested capital, the return is negative. Invested=1000, MarketValue=800, Dividends=0 → −20 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTotalReturn_Should_ReturnNull_When_InvestedCapitalIsZero
member.summary: When invested capital is zero the method must return null to avoid division by zero.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTotalReturn_Should_IncludeDividends_In_TotalReturn
member.summary: Dividends are added to the numerator, increasing the total return. Invested=1000, MarketValue=1000, Dividends=100 → 100/1000 = 10 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_ReturnNull_When_NoPeriods
member.summary: An empty period list must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_ReturnNull_When_FirstPeriodHasZeroDenominator
member.summary: When the only period has a zero denominator (StartValue=0, ExternalCashflow=0) the period is skipped and the method returns null because no valid periods remain.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_Calculate_SinglePeriod_Correctly
member.summary: Single period: StartValue=1000, EndValue=1100, ExternalCashflow=0 → TWR = 10 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_Compound_Multiple_Periods
member.summary: Two periods each returning +10 % compound to ≈ 21 % (1.1 × 1.1 − 1).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_SkipPeriod_When_InitialValue_IsZero
member.summary: When the first period has a zero denominator it is skipped; only the valid second period is used in the compounding chain.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_Handle_ExternalCashflows_Correctly
member.summary: Modified Dietz: StartValue=1000, EndValue=1150, ExternalCashflow=100. Denominator = 1000 + 0.5×100 = 1050. PeriodReturn = (1150−1000−100)/1050 ≈ 4.76 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_ReturnNull_When_NoSignChange
member.summary: All cashflows with the same sign (no sign change) → IRR cannot be computed → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_Calculate_SimpleReturn
member.summary: Simple buy-then-sell with 10 % gain over one year → IRR ≈ 10 %. Buy −1000 on 2020-01-01, Sell +1100 on 2021-01-01.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_Handle_DividendsInCashflows
member.summary: Dividends received during the holding period increase the IRR above zero. Buy −1000 on 2020-01-01, Dividend +50 after 6 months, Sell +1000 after 1 year → IRR > 0.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_ReturnNull_When_NotConverged
member.summary: A cashflow pattern that forces the bisection into a range with no sign change (e.g., huge positive terminal value dwarfing initial outflow for extreme rates) causes the algorithm to return null when it cannot converge. Uses maxIterations=1 to guarantee non-convergence even for solvable problems.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateCagr_Should_Calculate_TwoYearGrowth
member.summary: StartValue=1000, EndValue=1210, Years=2 → CAGR = √(1210/1000) − 1 = 10 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateCagr_Should_ReturnNull_When_StartValueIsZero
member.summary: StartValue=0 is invalid → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateCagr_Should_ReturnNull_When_YearsIsZero
member.summary: Years=0 is invalid → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateCagr_Should_Handle_NegativeReturn
member.summary: StartValue=1000, EndValue=810, Years=1 → CAGR = 810/1000 − 1 = −19 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_ReturnNull_When_LessThanTwoPrices
member.summary: Fewer than 2 price points → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_Calculate_ConstantPrices_ReturnZero
member.summary: Constant prices [100, 100, 100, 100] produce zero log returns → volatility = 0.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_Calculate_Positive_For_VariablePrices
member.summary: Non-constant prices produce a positive annualised volatility.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateMaxDrawdown_Should_ReturnNull_When_LessThanTwoPoints
member.summary: Fewer than 2 data points → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateMaxDrawdown_Should_Return_Zero_For_Monotonic_Increase
member.summary: A strictly monotonically increasing series has no drawdown → 0.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateMaxDrawdown_Should_ReturnNegative_Fraction
member.summary: Any drawdown must be expressed as a negative fraction (not positive, not > 0).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateSharpeRatio_Should_ReturnNull_When_VolatilityIsZero
member.summary: Volatility=0 → null (division by zero guard).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateSharpeRatio_Should_ReturnNegative_When_ReturnBelowRiskFreeRate
member.summary: When the portfolio return is below the risk-free rate the Sharpe Ratio is negative. Return=0.02, RiskFree=0.04, Volatility=0.10 → (0.02−0.04)/0.10 = −0.2.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateSharpeRatio_Should_ReturnZero_When_AnnualisedReturnEqualsRiskFreeRate
member.summary: When annualised return equals the risk-free rate, excess return is 0 → Sharpe Ratio = 0.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateDividendYield_Should_ReturnCorrectYield_When_DividendsAndCapitalArePositive
member.summary: Dividend yield = totalDividends / investedCapital. 50 / 1000 = 0.05 = 5 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateDividendYield_Should_ReturnNull_When_InvestedCapitalIsZero
member.summary: When invested capital is zero the method must return null to avoid division by zero.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateDividendYield_Should_ReturnZero_When_TotalDividendsIsZero
member.summary: When total dividends are zero the yield is exactly 0 (not null).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateDividendYield_Should_HandleNegativeDividends_When_DividendsAreNegative
member.summary: Negative dividends (e.g. withholding tax refund reversal) produce a negative yield.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTaxRate_Should_ReturnCorrectRate_When_TaxesAndGrossReturnArePositive
member.summary: Tax rate = totalTaxes / |grossReturn|. 20 / 100 = 20 %.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTaxRate_Should_ReturnNull_When_GrossReturnIsZero
member.summary: When gross return is zero the method must return null to avoid division by zero.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTaxRate_Should_ReturnZero_When_TotalTaxesIsZero
member.summary: When taxes are zero the tax rate is exactly 0 (not null).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTaxRate_Should_UseAbsoluteValue_When_GrossReturnIsNegative
member.summary: When gross return is negative (loss) the denominator uses |grossReturn|, so the tax rate is still a positive fraction.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTotalReturn_Should_ReduceReturn_When_NetDividendsAreNegative
member.summary: Negative net dividends (high withholding taxes) reduce total return below (MarketValue - InvestedCapital) / InvestedCapital. Invested=1000, MarketValue=1100, NetDividends=−20 → (1100 − 20 − 1000) / 1000 = 0.08.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_ReturnNull_When_ListHasExactlyOneCashflow
member.summary: A list with fewer than 2 cashflows must return null immediately.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_ReturnNull_When_ListIsEmpty
member.summary: An empty cashflow list must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_ReturnNull_When_InputIsNull
member.summary: A null input must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateIrr_Should_ReturnNull_When_CashflowsHaveNoSignChange
member.summary: When all cashflows have the same sign (no sign change), IRR is not computable → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateCagr_Should_ReturnNull_When_YearsIsNegative
member.summary: A negative time period is invalid → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateCagr_Should_ReturnNull_When_EndValueIsNegativeAndYearsIsNonInteger
member.summary: When endValue is negative and years is non-integer, Math.Pow returns NaN → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_ReturnNull_When_InputIsNull
member.summary: A null input must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_ReturnNull_When_AllPricesAreZeroOrNegative
member.summary: When all prices are zero or negative, the log-return computation is skipped for every pair (previous <= 0 or current <= 0). The underlying array stays at its default 0.0, which is neither NaN nor Infinity, so those entries are counted as valid and the method returns 0 (annualised volatility of zero returns) rather than null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_ReturnNull_When_ExactlyTwoPricesProvided
member.summary: BUG-2: The entry guard checks for fewer than 2 prices, but because only 2 prices produce exactly 1 log return (validCount = 1), the internal guard "validCount < 2" fires → null. This test documents the actual (surprising) behavior: 2 prices are NOT enough.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateVolatility_Should_ReturnNonNull_When_ExactlyThreePricesProvided
member.summary: Three prices produce 2 valid log returns (validCount == 2), which satisfies the internal guard. This is the effective minimum for a non-null result.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_ReturnNull_When_InputIsNull
member.summary: A null input must return null (guard before Count check).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateTwr_Should_ReturnNull_When_AllPeriodsHaveZeroDenominator
member.summary: When all periods have denominator == 0 (empty portfolio for all periods), no valid periods exist → null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateMaxDrawdown_Should_ReturnNull_When_InputIsNull
member.summary: A null input must return null.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateMaxDrawdown_Should_ReturnZero_When_AllValuesAreIdentical
member.summary: When all portfolio values are identical, the drawdown from peak to trough is 0.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Securities.ReturnCalculationServiceTests.CalculateMaxDrawdown_Should_ReturnZero_When_FirstValueIsZero
member.summary: When the first value is zero, the peak starts at 0 and the guard (peak > 0) prevents any drawdown from being recorded until a positive peak is established. With a monotonically non-decreasing series after the initial zero, no trough ever falls below the peak → max drawdown stays at 0.

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftImportSplitTests
member.summary: Tests the import split logic (CreateDraftAsync) for fixed, monthly and hybrid modes. NOTE: MinEntriesPerDraft (new feature) is NOT yet applied by the implementation – tests document current behaviour (TDD baseline).

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSecurityClassificationTests.CreateSecurityAccountAsync(FinanceManager.Infrastructure.AppDbContext,System.Guid)
member.summary: Creates a bank contact and an account with security processing enabled. Securities may only be auto-assigned when a detected account explicitly allows it.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSecurityClassificationTests.AutoAssignSecurity_ByIdentifier_SetsSecurityId
member.summary: Verifies that an entry whose subject contains the security identifier gets the security auto-assigned. A detected account with security processing enabled is required for auto-assignment.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSecurityClassificationTests.AutoAssignSecurity_ByNameWithUmlauts_SetsSecurityId
member.summary: Verifies that a security name containing umlauts is matched when the entry subject uses the ASCII equivalents (ue/oe/ae). A detected account with security processing enabled is required.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSecurityClassificationTests.AutoAssignSecurity_MultipleMatches_AssignsFirstByName_AndKeepsStatusOpen
member.summary: Verifies that when multiple securities match the entry subject, the first by name is assigned and the entry status remains Open to flag the ambiguity. A detected account with security processing enabled is required.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSplitLinkTests.BookAsync_ShouldUseParentBookingDateAndChildBookingAsValuta_WhenSplitDraftsLinked
member.summary: Ensures split-draft postings use the parent booking date and child booking dates as valuta dates.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSplitLinkTests.ClassifyAsync_ShouldKeepOpen_WhenSplitDraftEntryAmountDiffers
member.summary: Ensures split-draft entries are not marked as already booked when the amount differs.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftSplitLinkTests.ClassifyAsync_ShouldMarkAlreadyBooked_WhenSplitDraftEntryReimported
member.summary: Ensures split-draft duplicates are detected when re-importing the parent statement entry.

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.Statements.StatementDraftUploadGroupSplitTests
member.summary: Tests for booking split drafts that are grouped only by a shared UploadGroupId (multi-part upload) instead of a single explicit SplitDraftId link. Expected behaviour: - Parent draft has an intermediary entry (Contact.IsPaymentIntermediary = true). - User links ONE of the child drafts (same upload group) via SetEntrySplitDraft. - Validation and booking must treat ALL drafts of that upload group (without account + still Draft) as children. - Sum check compares parent entry amount with sum of ALL child draft entry amounts across the group. - Booking parent books all grouped child drafts (and commits them) while unrelated drafts remain untouched.

member.name: T:FinanceManager.Tests.TestHelpers.TestReturnAnalysisLocalizer
member.summary: Stub implementation of for unit tests. Returns the German (de) translation for every key so that existing string assertions continue to work without modification. Unknown keys fall back to the key itself.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.ViewModels.PostingsSecurityViewModelTests.GetQuantityCell(FinanceManager.Web.ViewModels.Postings.SecurityPostingsListViewModel,FinanceManager.Web.ViewModels.Common.ListRecord)
member.summary: Returns the Quantity cell from a record by looking up the column index.

member.name: M:FinanceManager.Tests.Web.SecurityPriceErrorRecoveryTests.NotificationService_ShouldNotClearPriceError_WhenDismissingSecurityErrorNotification
member.summary: Verifies that dismissing a notification only marks it as seen and does not clear the security error.
